sources.notes: Defines an alternating-sum objective plus swap cost; Alice can end or swap first, and Bob can end immediately after.
Official reference code checks the initial value, same-parity distance gain, and opposite-parity swap deltas with maintained parity minima.

annotation.summary: Given an array and a game where each move can either stop or swap two positions while adding distance to cost, compute the optimal final alternating-sum value.
annotation.constraints: Sum of n over tests is at most 2e5, so the optimal first move must be evaluated in linear time.
annotation.core_idea: Bob can stop after Alice's first swap, so Alice only needs to maximize the initial alternating sum after at most one beneficial swap. Same-parity swaps only add distance, while opposite-parity swaps change the alternating sum by twice the moved values plus distance; maintain the best previous even/odd expression to test every right endpoint.

solution_variants.name: one-swap-parity-greedy
solution_variants.summary: Compute the baseline alternating sum, then scan positions while maintaining minimum expressions for previous even and odd positions to maximize the one-swap delta.

tags.evidence: The key game observation is that Bob's ability to end immediately reduces the minimax game to Alice choosing no swap or exactly one first swap.
After the game collapse, Alice greedily takes the maximum candidate delta among same-parity distance gains and opposite-parity swap gains.
The scan keeps the minimum even/odd transformed contribution so each possible second endpoint is evaluated in O(1).
